steps.title: Read two intercepts from the graph
steps.content: Do not expand every answer choice first. The graph gives two clean points: the $y$-intercept $(0,40)$ and the $x$-intercept $(60,0)$. A correct equation must pass through both.

steps.title: Fit those points in Desmos
steps.content: Enter the two intercepts as a table and run a linear regression. Desmos gives slope $m=-\frac{2}{3}$ and intercept $b=40$, so the line is $$y=-\frac{2}{3}x+40.$$

steps.title: Convert to match the choices
steps.content: Rewrite $y=-\frac{2}{3}x+40$ in standard form. Multiply by $3$: $3y=-2x+120$. Move $2x$ to the left: $2x+3y=120$. Multiplying by $4$ gives the equivalent equation $$8x+12y=480.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer choice
steps.content: Choice B is $8x+12y=480$, and it passes through both visible intercepts. Choice A and C have positive slope, so they cannot match the downward line. Choice D gives the wrong $y$-intercept. Choice B is correct.
